canonical_definition: The specific action of assigning a numerical score, typically on a Likert scale, to a debate speech to quantify its quality.
papers: BSFA: Leveraging the Subspace Dichotomy to Accelerate Neural Network Training

definitions.paper: BSFA: Leveraging the Subspace Dichotomy to Accelerate Neural Network Training

definitions.text: Producing quality assessments of debate speeches along dimensions such as persuasiveness, coherence, and argument strength, based on input speech content.

characterization: The specific action of assigning a numerical score, typically on a Likert scale, to a debate speech to quantify its quality.
